title: Information & Communication Technology
description: Equips students with essential digital skills including computer hardware and software, information systems, file management, and office applications. Strong foundation for further IT studies.
languages: Digital Literacy, Office Apps, File Management

title: Information Technology
description: Introduces basic computing and technology concepts including number systems, communications, computer architecture, and programming basics. Builds algorithmic thinking and data processing skills.

title: Programming with Python
description: Learn one of the world's most popular programming languages. Covers variables, conditions, loops, functions, and efficiency concepts — ideal for beginners and future AP Computer Science Principles students.

title: Programming with Java
description: Basic introduction to Java building a foundation for AP Computer Science A. Students learn data types, logic, control structures, iterative processes, and objects — includes weekly lab work.
prerequisites: B+ in CSE202 or Algebra II, or department permission

title: Web Design & Internet
description: Explore web design using HTML, CSS, and responsive design techniques while discovering how the Internet works — its architecture, protocols, and societal impact.

title: Introduction to Artificial Intelligence
description: Engaging introduction to AI covering machine learning, natural language processing, neural networks, and computer vision with hands-on experience. Critically explores ethical implications of AI.
prerequisites: CSE302, CSE303, or CSE304 with B+ or higher

title: AP Computer Science A
description: Year-long AP course focused on object-oriented programming in Java. Covers common algorithms, built-in classes, modularity, and problem-solving. Students are expected to take the AP CS A exam in May.
prerequisites: B+ in any Math course AND B+ in CSE202, CSE302, or CSE304

title: AP Computer Science Principles
description: Introduces foundational components of computer science and computational thinking. Students develop innovative computation artifacts and explore the vital impacts computing has on people and society.
prerequisites: B+ in any English AND B+ in any Math AND A in CSE202, CSE304, or CSE302

title: Introduction to CAD
description: Develop foundational Computer-Aided Design skills for 2D and 3D modeling. Topics include creation of parts, assemblies, and drawings using professional CAD software.

title: Design Thinking
description: Foster creativity, empathy, and problem-solving through hands-on experiences tackling real-world challenges. Prepares students for EGR309 & EGR403 and cultivates an innovative mindset.

title: Programming with Electronics
description: Introduces electronics basics using Arduino micro-computers along with manufacturing tools (soldering, laser cutting, 3D printing). Delivers interdisciplinary learning across Engineering, Science, and Art.

title: Robotics Engineering
description: Design, fabricate, assemble, and program competition robots for FIRST and VEX Robotics competitions. Students learn the Engineering Design Process, teamwork, and real-world robotics applications.
